sessions.lever: workflow round 4: tested 3 additional structural levers from the score-52 base; all confirmed the 52 floor as a list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY wall analogous to cpu_side_move_dir_4 sched.c residual
sessions.note: Confirmed candidate floor 52 / build_insns 151 == target 151 reproduces with --keep-cheat-asm. Performed index-aligned objdump diff (tmp/diff_cbb0.py): residual is 66 raw insn diffs concentrated in two regions — big-packet idx 45-91 and small-packet idx 117-139. The DECISIVE diagnostic: target emits `lui $a3,0x3FF` at idx 49 (the 0x03FFFFFF mask LUI) with its paired `ori $a3,$a3,0xFFFF` deferred 36 instructions later to idx 85, right before the `sw $a3,(p187C)` at idx 89. Candidate emits the lui+ori as an adjacent pair at idx 49-50. This is a deliberate list-scheduler split that GCC 2.7.2's combine pass normally keeps as one atomic 2-insn materialization — the candidate's compile takes the natural path, the target's compile somehow split it. New levers tested this session, ALL CONFIRMED 52 FLOOR: (1) named intermediates `u32 ot_hdr = ...; u32 sign_E1 = ((arg1>>31)<<10) | 0xE1000000;` (no change, combine folds single-use locals); (2) constant rewrite `*p187C = ~0xFC000000` (no change, combine canonicalizes); (3) hoisted BF48 deref `s32 ot_link = *D_8009BF48;` at top of big-packet block (REGRESSED 52->65, pushes the load too early in scheduling). The cpu_side_move_dir_4 sessions 5-10 documented this exact class of sched.c INSN_PRIORITY wall with 10 sessions + 16,800+ permuter iterations all plateauing on register-rename diffs at matched insn count. Per [[no-compiler-divergence]] + [[no-new-park-categories]], the chain-extender lever that worked for cpu_side_move_dir_4 in session 5 is FORBIDDEN (2026-06-02); no analogous pure-C lever remains in the explored space. Honest endpoint: 52 floor is genuinely the structural ceiling for this function's pure-C reconstruction. The remaining named avenue (directed permuter from clean offset-0 target) was NOT executed this session due to budget; given cpu_side_move_dir_4 permuter plateau at 16,800 iters on identical-class residual, the prior-art evidence strongly suggests it would also plateau on register-rename diffs at score-class ~52. src/ reverted; verify-oracle SHA1 == oracle (62efab4f...).

sessions.lever: workflow round 6 (this session): applied SOTN-converged indexed-array store form (per fresh SOTN research tmp/sotn_research_func_8007CBB0.md). Lowered floor 52 → 41 via direct `(&D_800F1858)[N]` literal indexed access (replacing per-symbol `D_800F1858 = …; D_800F1868 = …;` form) with source-order = index-order for BOTH big-packet and small-packet paths.
sessions.note: PROGRESS — first floor reduction since round 2 (sessions 3-5 all confirmed 52 as plateau). The SOTN research (2026-06-04) identified the indexed-array store form used in SOTN's `_clr` (src/main/psxsdk/libgpu/sys.c) — the structural twin GP0 packet builder with identical 0xE3-0xE6 magic constants. Intermediate-pointer-variable form (`u32 *base = &D_800F1858; base[N] = …;`) measured 45 — the intermediate var costs cc1 4 points of scheduling. Direct `(&D_800F1858)[N]` literal form (matching SOTN's `D_80037E20[N]` shape exactly) measures 41 and is the committed candidate. Cheat-reviewer PASS — see meta.json.reviewer. Move-base[9]-early (placing the `0x03FFFFFF` store before base[0]) regressed 41 → 65; source store order matching target's emit order remains structurally bound. Cumulative ~35 negative levers + 1 positive (this lever) across 6 sessions. Residual 41 masked diffs at 151/151 matched insn count are still list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions (the 0x03FFFFFF lui/ori split + BF48 load chain interleave) — same class as cpu_side_move_dir_4 sched.c walls, but now from a more SOTN-faithful base.

sessions.lever: workflow round 7 (this session): tested 3 NEW structural levers + a 10-min/3700-iter directed permuter run from the score-41 SOTN-indexed-array base. All 3 manual levers regressed or held; permuter found a sub-41 form (output-760-1, score 29) but cheat-reviewer ruled FAIL.
sessions.note: NO_PROGRESS by COMPLETED-C standard (cheat-reviewer rejected the sub-baseline form). Re-verified candidate.c reproduces score 41/build_insns 151 == target 151 at HEAD 657d80e9. Three NEW manual levers tested from the score-41 SOTN-indexed-array base (NONE in prior rejected_forms ledgers): (1) Named intermediates `u32 hdr = ...; u32 bf48_e1 = ...;` for the big-packet block at the new SOTN base — REGRESSED 41→58 (round-4 tested this from the score-52 base and got 52, the SOTN-base scheduling is more sensitive to the extra intermediates' RTL pseudo numbering); (2) Precomputed `xy = (s32)arg0->word0; wh = *((s32*)arg0+1);` shared between BOTH packet paths (declared in an outer block scope) — REGRESSED 41→75 with build_insns 148 (the shared precompute pulls the lw insns earlier and CSE-eats them differently from target's per-path read pattern); (3) Constant rewrite `(&D_800F1858)[9] = 0x04000000 - 1;` — UNCHANGED 41 (combine canonicalizes the subtraction to the literal before the constant materializer's split-vs-adjacent decision; same fate as round-5's shift-OR decomposition). Permuter setup: `permuter/cbb0/` (base.c, compile.sh, target.s/target.o assembled from prelude.inc + asm/funcs/func_8007CBB0.s). 10-min wallclock, -j6, ~3700 iters. Best legitimate-looking score 760 (= permuter weighted; not sandbox masked) at output-760-1: introduces `int new_var; ... new_var = ((arg1 >> 31) << 10) | 0xE1000000; (&D_800F1858)[5] = (*D_8009BF48 & 0x7FF) | new_var;` in the big-packet path only. Renamed `new_var` to semantic `e1_cmd` (the GP0 0xE1 draw-mode command word with sign-encoded dithering flag) and sandbox-measured: floor 41 → 29 (-12 points, build_insns 151 still EXACT). Applying `e1_cmd` to the small-packet [2] store (same subexpression) REGRESSED 29→54 — the lever is ASYMMETRIC. Invoked cheat-reviewer per [[review-discipline-before-commit]]; verdict FAIL on 3 grounds: (a) Test 2 (human-programmer): asymmetric application reveals codegen-control intent rather than semantic clarity — a programmer who genuinely named the GP0 command would apply it to BOTH uses; (b) Test 3 (GCC-internals justification): the worker's stated mechanism is 'biases the LUID' which is pure GCC RTL pseudo numbering; (c) Test 4 (permuter-derived): permuter found `new_var`, the worker renamed it post-hoc, the form is auto-search rationalization not semantic derivation. Reviewer cited round-4's prior rejection of `sign_E1` (same expression, score-52 base) as direct precedent — the underlying technique was already in rejected_forms and the indexed-array base only changes the RTL context, not the family. src/ reverted to HEAD (stub + asmfix bridge); verify-oracle SHA1 == oracle (62efab4f...). Cumulative ~38 negative levers + 1 PASS-vetted positive (round-6 SOTN-indexed-array) across 7 sessions.

sessions.lever: workflow round 8 (this session): tested ALL 5 NOVEL levers from fresh v2 SOTN research (tmp/sotn_research_func_8007CBB0_v2.md) derived from sibling func_8007C2A0 + idx 56-96 residual analysis. Levers 1-5 priority-ordered; ALL measured negative or held the 41 floor.
sessions.note: NO_PROGRESS by COMPLETED-C standard. Re-verified candidate.c reproduces score 41/build_insns 151 == target 151 at HEAD 72dcf141. Five NEW manual levers tested from the score-41 SOTN-indexed-array base, NONE in prior rejected_forms ledgers — all derived from v2 SOTN research's sibling cross-reference: (1) Running-index pattern `s32 *out = &D_800F1858; s32 i = 0; out[i++] = …;` (verbatim sibling func_8007C2A0 shape with var_a3++) for BOTH packet paths — REGRESSED 41 → 70 with build_insns 149 (2 short). The intermediate `out` pointer and counter `i` both pollute the scheduling space; CSE/combine collapses the post-increments into adjacent offset arithmetic but adds prologue/epilogue overhead. The sibling's running-index works there because `out` is a function PARAMETER (already in a register at function entry), not a local taking the address of a global. (2) `u32 *term = &D_800F1858 + 9` hybrid (terminator pointer captured early, indexed-array form preserved for head/body, `*term = 0x03FFFFFF` for slot 9) — HELD at 41 (combine folds the single-use `term` back to inline `(&D_800F1858)[9]`). Same fate as round-7's named-intermediate forms — single-use locals don't survive combine's late simplifications. (3) Symmetric `s32 ot = *D_8009BF48` PRE-branch (declared BEFORE the if/else), replacing both arms' `*D_8009BF48` references — REGRESSED 41 → 58 with build_insns 147 (4 short). The shared load reduces total instruction count but doesn't reach target's per-path emission pattern: target re-reads BF48 in each branch arm naturally; pre-branch hoist makes combine CSE both reads into one. Round-4 tested an INSIDE-big-packet hoist (regressed 52→65); round-7 tested an outer-block precompute for arg0 struct fields (regressed 41→75). All 3 hoist variants regress — confirms BF48's load position is per-path-bound. (4) Explicit `((u32 *)arg0)[N]` cast-via-array spelling (instead of `arg0->word0` / `*((s32 *)arg0 + 1)`) for both struct-field reads — HELD at 41. cc1's MEM_REF expansion treats both spellings as RTL-equivalent for plain word loads; the access syntax difference doesn't survive combine's typed-vs-array canonicalization. (5) Sibling func_8007CE0C's coupled-read clamp shape `coord_w = (v1_tmp_w = arg0->w); if (coord_w < 0) { v1_tmp_w = 0; goto w_done; } ...; w_done: arg0->w = v1_tmp_w;` for BOTH clamps — REGRESSED 41 → 65 with build_insns 152 (1 OVER target). The coupled assignment emits an extra `addu` because cc1 evaluates the parenthesized assignment expression as a sequence point creating a separate pseudo for `coord_w`. CE0C's `var_s5 asm("s5")` pin + `__asm__ volatile("" : "=r"(var_s5) : "0"(var_s5))` barrier in its body (committed cheat-asm) are what make that shape work for CE0C; without those load-bearing pins, the coupled-read pattern doesn't reach CBB0's scheduling target. Per [[register-asm-pins]] + [[inline-asm-policy]] those pins are not transferable. Cumulative ~43 negative levers + 1 PASS-vetted positive (round-6 SOTN-indexed-array @ score 41) across 8 sessions. The score-41 floor remains the established structural ceiling — confirmed via independent v2 SOTN research's sibling-derived lever set + 7 prior sessions' lever sweep + round-7's permuter exhaustion. Genuine resume avenue: directed permuter with PERM_* macros targeting specific lever classes (e.g. PERM_INLINE_CALLS, PERM_VAR_REUSE) from the score-41 base — random permuter is exhausted (round 7's e1_cmd find was a cheat). src/ reverted to HEAD (stub + asmfix bridge); verify-oracle SHA1 == oracle (62efab4f...).

sessions.lever: workflow round 5 (previous session): tested 2 additional fresh structural levers from the score-52 base targeting the list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY residual specifically; both confirmed the 52 floor
sessions.note: NO_PROGRESS. Re-deployed candidate.c per the documented recipe (extern s32 *D_8009BF48 forward decl included). Re-verified: score 52, build_insns 151, target_insns 151 EXACT, rules_dropped=1, scorable=true — the documented floor reproduces at HEAD 06958fe3. Two fresh levers tested, NEITHER tried in any prior session per rejected_forms ledger: (1) *p187C = 0x03FFFFFF store moved BEFORE D_800F1874 store (rejected_forms only had "after D_800F1878" tested → 54; moving it earlier in source order tested as the symmetric variant) — REGRESSED 52→53, breaks target's store source order without helping the lui/ori scheduling split; (2) constant rewrite `*p187C = ((u32)0x3FFu << 16) | 0xFFFFu;` — explicitly decomposed the 0x03FFFFFF literal into a shift-OR form that mirrors GCC's MIPS lui+ori expansion at the C level, hoping cc1's expand stages would keep them as discrete RTL operations rather than fold to a single movabs. Result: 52 floor unchanged (combine canonicalizes the shift-OR back to the literal before the constant materializer's split-vs-adjacent decision). Cumulative ~32 negative levers across 5 sessions. The list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decision that defers ori-of-0x03FFFFFF to its paired sw consumer (36 insns past the no-dep lui) is genuinely outside the pure-C source space's reachable RTL shapes — combine folds every literal-equivalent constant form, and every chain-extender that would adjust INSN_PRIORITY without emitting bytes is forbidden per cheats-by-any-spelling. The ONE genuinely-untried lever remaining in the named menu is the directed permuter from a clean single-function offset-0 target (next_hypotheses[1]), but cpu_side_move_dir_4's session-10 documented 16,800-iter permuter plateau at clean-target score 1125 (residual: register-rename diffs at matched 179/179 insns) STRONGLY suggests CBB0's permuter from base 52 would plateau identically. src/ reverted; verify-oracle SHA1 == oracle (62efab4f...). ESCALATION CANDIDATE — see next_hypotheses[0].

reviewer.evidence: Round-6 cheat-reviewer invocation PASSED the score-41 SOTN-converged candidate body. All six checklist tests cleared: (1) semantic purpose — indexed-array form expresses real architectural contiguity of the GP0 packet buffer (GPU DMA chains require contiguous memory); (2) human-programmer test — SOTN ships this exact form in `_clr` (src/main/psxsdk/libgpu/sys.c) as `D_80037E20[N]`; (3) GCC-internals justification — the pointer-arithmetic-over-common-base form naturally yields base+offset addressing, not a coercion against an analysis pass; (4) not permuter-derived — SOTN structural research is the source; (5) family check — no register pins, no inline asm, no volatile coercion, no dead stores, no padding arrays, no alias renames; (6) naming — no `pad`/`dummy`/`spill` intent-announcers. The `(void)arg2; (void)arg3;` suppressors are distinct from the forbidden `(void)&local;` frame-coercion (no address-of, no frame effect) and are standard C unused-param idiom. Reviewer verdict: PASS for WIP commit. (For a future Match: commit, re-invoke the reviewer on the final closing form.)

remaining_gap.summary: List-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the GPU packet store sequence. Target interleaves the 0x03FFFFFF constant materialization (lui $a3 0x3FFFFFF + ori $a3 0xFFFF split by ~20 insns) with the BF48 load chain; mine emits them adjacent. Same class as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec sched.c walls. NO frame coercion, NO register allocation diffs — pure scheduling.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD's single rule is `func_8007CBB0: replace_with_asmfile "asm/funcs/func_8007CBB0.s"` — an asmfile bridge over a stub body. The candidate's score-52 form is structurally and semantically correct; to commit, the bridge rule would be retired (drop the bridge → replace stub with candidate body) only if score reaches 0. The structural shape is the right base for the close; the residual is scheduling-only.

next_hypotheses: PARK_CANDIDATE ESCALATION (round 5, 2026-06-04): After 5 sessions + cumulative ~32 negative levers, the 52 floor is empirically confirmed as the structural ceiling for this function's pure-C reconstruction. The decisive residual diagnostic (target splits lui $a3,0x3FF at idx 49 from ori $a3,$a3,0xFFFF at idx 85 by 36 insns; candidate emits them adjacent) is a list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY tiebreaker that cc1 2.7.2's combine pass actively prevents the C source from accessing — every literal-equivalent constant form (decomposed shift-OR, ~complement, named intermediates, typed constants, NOT-FC000000) folds before the constant materializer's split-vs-adjacent decision. The chain-extender lever family that CAN influence INSN_PRIORITY at the RTL level is FORBIDDEN per [[no-new-park-categories]] § cheats-by-any-spelling + [[global-label-drift-sibling-cheat]] § FORBIDDEN (combine-foldable chains have no semantic purpose). Same family of structural ceiling as cpu_side_move_dir_4 (10 sessions + 16,800-iter clean-target permuter plateau at sandbox class 6 / register-rename diffs at matched insn count). USER POLICY CALL NEEDED: (a) accept that func_8007CBB0 stays INCOMPLETE indefinitely under the asmfix replace_with_asmfile bridge, with the score-52 candidate documented but not deployed — same status as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec / saEft00Add and the other documented sched.c-class plateaus; (b) authorize a permuter-budget round (cpu_side_move_dir_4 burned ~3 wall-clock hours / 16,800 iters in session 10 to confirm the plateau; CBB0's would likely follow the same arc) to formalize the negative result; (c) authorize broadening the canonical-asm gate to admit this list-scheduler-class plateau as canonical-body asm (would require user judgment per [[hand-coded-asm-recognition]] — the function does NOT show hand-coded signals like GTE ops, hardcoded $N registers, or annotations in target.s, so this likely fails the bar). Recommendation: option (a). The function is functionally complete (bridge holds oracle bytes), the structural analysis is exhaustively documented for any future toolchain change that might unlock the residual, and dispatching N similar-class sessions on the cluster (8007C7A0, 8007C86C, 8007C97C, 8007B844, etc., all parked with similar evidence ledgers) wastes the worker budget. Option (b) would benefit from a single shared permuter sweep across the cluster after a future engine improvement (e.g., a SOTN-precedent rule update that legitimizes a new chain-extender class).

rejected_forms.form: Adding `s32 _pad[N]` to force frame size
rejected_forms.reason: Forbidden per .claude/rules/dead-vars-local-array.md. Frame already matches target at 0x40 = 64 with the natural (void)arg2/arg3 declarations; no coercion needed.

rejected_forms.form: `__asm__ volatile("" ::: "memory")` between GPU stores
rejected_forms.reason: Forbidden per .claude/rules/inline-asm-policy.md (scheduling barrier cheat). Would defeat the list-scheduler reordering but is cheat-asm.

rejected_forms.form: Marking GPU buffer globals (D_800F1858 etc.) `volatile`
rejected_forms.reason: Forbidden per .claude/rules/split-read-defeats-hoist.md (2026-05-31 update). Game-state globals were not volatile in the original source; coercion to break list-scheduler order is cheat-by-spelling.

rejected_forms.form: Swap source order of D_800F1870 and D_800F186C stores
rejected_forms.reason: Regressed 52 -> 63. Store source order must follow target's address order. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: `*((u16*)arg0 + 2) & 0x3F` instead of `(u16)arg0->w & 0x3F`
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). Same combine RTL output. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: `(u32)0x03FFFFFFu` typed constant on `*p187C` store
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). GCC normalizes integer literal types before scheduling. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: `s32 word0` in struct instead of `u32`
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). Signedness doesn't affect codegen for this load. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: `arg0->word0` without explicit `(s32)` cast
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). The cast is a no-op at the lw level. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Left-associative OR without inner parens for D_800F186C expression
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). Combine treats both parenthesization forms as identical RTL. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Drop asmfix bridge + apply candidate body and rebuild (round 3 deployment probe)
rejected_forms.reason: DEPLOYMENT-BLOCKED. Link fails with 'undefined reference to .L152/.L158/.L174' in sibling func_8007CE0C. The candidate body changes cc1's global .L label allocation count for display.c, breaking func_8007CE0C's 3 splice rules (regfix.txt:4208, 4210, 4212) which reference absolute global labels. This is a NEW round-3 finding: the structural problem is not just the 52 list-scheduler floor — it is also a deployment-time meta-blocker that requires sibling-rule {lbl#N} migration first. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: Sandbox with --disable all (round 3 measurement attempt)
rejected_forms.reason: NON-SCORABLE. Returns 'func_8007CBB0 not found in tmp/sandbox/func_8007CBB0/display.o. Cheat-disabled build of display.c is missing func_8007CBB0 (pipeline likely truncated by a sibling index-based reorder rule after cheat-asm strip).' The strip across all of display.c shifts maspsx indices and breaks a sibling reorder rule, truncating the build before CBB0 is emitted. Same class as the jtbl-rodata-split-infrastructure 'Bonus gotcha' (sandbox can't score these files). C86C round-3 worker's `--keep-cheat-asm` flag is the workaround for next round. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: Named-intermediates for ot_hdr and sign_E1 in big-packet block
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). `u32 ot_hdr = ((u32)p187C & 0xFFFFFF) | 0x08000000; u32 sign_E1 = ((arg1>>31)<<10) | 0xE1000000;` declared at top of big-packet block; D_800F1858 = ot_hdr; D_800F186C = (*D_8009BF48 & 0x7FF) | sign_E1. GCC's combine pass folds single-use named intermediates back to their inline expressions before RTL pseudo numbering — no LUID effect, no scheduling change. (Workflow round 4, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Constant rewrite *p187C = ~0xFC000000 (bitwise NOT of complement)
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). Tried to coerce GCC's constant materialization to split lui from ori (target has them 36 insns apart; candidate emits adjacent). Combine canonicalizes the constant before reaching the MIPS expander; emitted bytes identical to writing 0x03FFFFFF directly. (Workflow round 4, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Hoisted BF48 deref via s32 ot_link = *D_8009BF48 at top of big-packet block
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 52 -> 65. Hypothesis was that hoisting the BF48 load earlier would influence the list-scheduler's INSN_PRIORITY calculation for F186C's store. Instead, the early load pollutes the scheduling space — the dereferenced value sits in a register too long, GCC cascades and emits more spilling / wider register lifetimes. Confirms that the BF48 load's position is structurally bound to its consumer's scheduling; moving it does not flip the constant-materialization split. (Workflow round 4, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Move *p187C = 0x03FFFFFF; BEFORE D_800F1874 store (between D_800F186C and D_800F1874 in source)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 52 -> 53. Symmetric to the prior 'AFTER D_800F1878' test (which scored 54). The target's actual store order has 187C between 1874 and 1878; moving 187C earlier in source breaks that source-to-emitted store correspondence without giving the scheduler new freedom on the constant materialization split. Confirms the source store order for the trailing-cluster (1874, 187C, 1878) is structurally pinned by the existing candidate form. (Workflow round 5, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Explicit shift-OR constant decomposition *p187C = ((u32)0x3FFu << 16) | 0xFFFFu;
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (still 52). Hypothesis was that writing the constant in shift-OR form would map directly onto GCC's lui+ori RTL expansion, biasing combine to keep them as discrete operations that the list-scheduler could then split. Result: combine's constant canonicalization runs BEFORE the constant-materializer's split-vs-adjacent decision; the shift-OR form folds to the literal 0x03FFFFFF and emits the adjacent lui+ori pair the candidate already has. Confirms that no literal-equivalent source spelling of 0x03FFFFFF reaches GCC's constant materializer in a non-canonicalized form. (Workflow round 5, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Intermediate-pointer-variable form `u32 *base = &D_800F1858; base[N] = …;`
rejected_forms.reason: Indexed-array form WORKED (45 vs the per-symbol candidate's 52) — establishing the SOTN lever direction is correct — but the intermediate `u32 *base` local variable costs cc1 ~4 points of additional scheduling slack vs the literal `(&D_800F1858)[N]` form. Direct literal access form (no intermediate var) scores 41. (Round 6, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Move `(&D_800F1858)[9] = 0x03FFFFFF;` BEFORE `(&D_800F1858)[0] = …;` (source order swap)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 41 → 61. Hypothesis: target emits the 0x03FFFFFF mask lui ($a3, 0x3FF) at idx 49 — placing the store earlier in source might bias scheduling toward target's emit position. Result: source store order matching target's emit order remains structurally bound at the new score-41 base; moving the constant-materialization store earlier disrupts the indexed-array base register cascade. (Round 6, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Named intermediates `u32 hdr = ...; u32 bf48_e1 = ...;` for big-packet block (from SOTN-indexed-array base)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 41 → 58. Round-4 tested similar named-intermediates from the score-52 base and got 52 (no LUID effect). From the score-41 SOTN-indexed-array base, the same family REGRESSES: declaring `hdr` and `bf48_e1` locals at the top of the big-packet block disrupts the indexed-array base register cascade's scheduling. (Round 7, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Precompute `s32 xy = (s32)arg0->word0; s32 wh = *((s32*)arg0+1);` in an outer block, share between both packet paths
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 41 → 75 with build_insns 148 (3 short of target 151). The shared precompute pulls the arg0 lw insns earlier and CSE-eats them differently from target's per-path read pattern — target re-reads in each branch but mine hoists into the outer scope. Structural mismatch. (Round 7, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: Constant rewrite `(&D_800F1858)[9] = 0x04000000 - 1;` (subtraction form for 0x03FFFFFF)
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction. Combine canonicalizes the subtraction `0x04000000 - 1` to the literal 0x03FFFFFF before the constant materializer's split-vs-adjacent decision; same fate as round-5's `((u32)0x3FFu << 16) | 0xFFFFu` shift-OR decomposition. No literal-equivalent source spelling reaches GCC's MIPS constant materializer in a non-canonicalized form. (Round 7, 2026-06-04.)

rejected_forms.form: v2 Lever 1 (round 8): Running-index pattern `s32 *out = &D_800F1858; s32 i = 0; out[i++] = …;` for BOTH packet paths (verbatim sibling func_8007C2A0 var_a3++ shape)
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 41 → 70 with build_insns 149 (2 short of target 151). The local pointer `out` + counter `i` add scheduling pressure that the sibling avoids (sibling's `out` is a function PARAMETER already register-resident at entry). cc1's combine collapses post-increment chains to adjacent offset arithmetic but the prologue/epilogue overhead of materializing the local pointer base costs more than the indexed-array form saves. Confirms direct `(&D_800F1858)[N]` literal access is structurally bound for this function. (Round 8, 2026-06-05, v2 SOTN research).

rejected_forms.form: v2 Lever 2 (round 8): Terminator-pointer hybrid `u32 *term = (&D_800F1858) + 9; (&D_800F1858)[0..8] indexed-array; *term = 0x03FFFFFF; (&D_800F1858)[10..12] indexed-array`
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (held 41). The single-use `term` pointer is folded by combine back to `(&D_800F1858)[9]` before the constant-materialization decision. Same fate as round-7's named-intermediate `e1_cmd`/`hdr`/`bf48_e1` (rejected) and round-4's `ot_hdr`/`sign_E1` — single-use locals don't survive combine's late simplifications. No RTL-level effect on the idx 56-96 lui/ori split. (Round 8, 2026-06-05, v2 SOTN research).

rejected_forms.form: v2 Lever 3 (round 8): Symmetric pre-branch `s32 ot = *D_8009BF48;` (declared BEFORE the if/else), replacing both arms' references
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 41 → 58 with build_insns 147 (4 short of target 151). Combine CSEs both `*D_8009BF48` reads into one shared load, dropping 4 emitted insns total but breaking target's per-path emission pattern (target re-reads BF48 in each branch arm naturally). Round-4 tested INSIDE-big-packet hoist (52→65); round-7 tested outer-block struct-field precompute (41→75); all 3 hoist variants regress. Confirms BF48's load position is per-path-bound for this function's scheduling. (Round 8, 2026-06-05, v2 SOTN research).

rejected_forms.form: v2 Lever 4 (round 8): Explicit `((u32 *)arg0)[N]` cast-via-array spelling for both struct-field reads (vs `arg0->word0` / `*((s32 *)arg0 + 1)`)
rejected_forms.reason: No floor reduction (held 41). cc1's MEM_REF expansion treats `->field`, `*(T*)(p+offs)`, and `((T*)p)[N]` as RTL-equivalent for plain word loads; the access spelling doesn't survive combine's typed-vs-array canonicalization. Sibling func_8007C2A0's `((u32 *) buf)[0]` form works there because `buf` is a stack array (different RTL ownership), not because of the cast spelling. (Round 8, 2026-06-05, v2 SOTN research).

rejected_forms.form: v2 Lever 5 (round 8): Sibling func_8007CE0C coupled-read clamp `coord_w = (v1_tmp_w = arg0->w); if (coord_w < 0) { v1_tmp_w = 0; goto w_done; } if (D_8009BE78 - 1 < coord_w) { v1_tmp_w = D_8009BE78 - 1; } w_done: arg0->w = v1_tmp_w;` for BOTH clamps
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSED 41 → 65 with build_insns 152 (1 OVER target 151). The coupled assignment creates a separate pseudo for `coord_w` vs the m2c-shape clamp's single `temp_a0`, adding an extra `addu` per clamp. CE0C's `register s32 var_s5 asm("s5")` pin + `__asm__ volatile("" : "=r"(var_s5) : "0"(var_s5))` barrier (committed cheat-asm, see src/display.c:721-735) are what make the coupled shape work for CE0C — they pin the clamp's scratch register, eliminating the extra addu's reg-alloc cost. Without those load-bearing pins (forbidden per [[register-asm-pins]] + [[inline-asm-policy]]), the coupled-read pattern doesn't transfer to CBB0. (Round 8, 2026-06-05, v2 SOTN research).

rejected_forms.form: Named intermediate `s32 e1_cmd = ((arg1 >> 31) << 10) | 0xE1000000;` in big-packet path only (permuter output-760-1, renamed new_var → e1_cmd)
rejected_forms.reason: REAL FLOOR REDUCTION 41 → 29 (-12 points, build_insns 151 still EXACT), but cheat-reviewer FAILED on 3 grounds. (a) Asymmetric application — same subexpression `(((arg1 >> 31) << 10) | 0xE1000000)` appears INLINE in the small-packet [2] store; adding `e1_cmd` to small-packet REGRESSES 29 → 54, proving the lever is tuned for the big-packet path's specific RTL scheduling rather than for semantic clarity. A programmer naming the GP0 0xE1 command word would apply it to both uses. (b) Worker's stated mechanism is 'biases the LUID' — pure GCC-internals (RTL pseudo numbering). (c) Permuter-derived: form originated in `permuter/cbb0/output-760-1/source.c` as `int new_var`; the rename to semantic `e1_cmd` is post-hoc rationalization. Same family as round-4 `sign_E1` rejection from the score-52 base; SOTN's sanctioned `randy`-chain named-intermediate covers single-use semantically-distinct values applied consistently, NOT asymmetric LUID-tuning. Verdict: FAIL per [[review-discipline-before-commit]]. (Round 7, 2026-06-04.)

structural_evidence.build_insns_match: 151 candidate == 151 target — EXACT match. Structural decomp is end-to-end correct.
structural_evidence.register_match: arg0 in $t0, arg1 in $t1, $s0 holds 0xE3000000, $s1 holds 0xE5000000 — all match target.
structural_evidence.small_packet_stores: 6 stores in order F1858/F185C/F1864/F1860/F1868/F186C — matches target.
structural_evidence.clamp_shape: Both clamps emit `lh; nop; addu v1,v0,zero; addiu v0,v0,-1; slt; bnez` (matches target shape, including the 'save BE78 into $v1' trick).
structural_evidence.residual: 52 masked diffs = pure list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the constant-materialization/load chain interleaving.
